slice: Resolve the repository-wide CM-0010 namespace collision without deleting either historical claim

memory_recovered.canonical_rule: Continuity IDs are repository-wide semantic keys across sanctioned storage roots, not directory-local labels.
observed: operations/continuity/records/CM-0010.json predates and canonically identifies the replay-protection evolution claim.
the peer-trigger layering claim was later created with the same identifier in operations/continuity/memory.
the current validator scans both records and memory roots and rejects duplicate IDs.
inferred: The least destructive repair is to retain the earlier canonical CM-0010 assignment and migrate the later peer-trigger claim to a fresh identifier.
proposed: Future continuity writers should allocate IDs against the aggregate repository namespace before committing.
superseded: CM-0010 as the identifier for the peer-trigger layering claim is superseded by CM-0013.
unresolved: The repository validator was not executed in this connector-only run, so full-tree validation remains to be retained as runtime evidence.

verification.static: The post-migration file has canonical id CM-0013, retains legacy_id CM-0010, cites the pre-migration blob, and leaves operations/continuity/records/CM-0010.json unchanged.
verification.expected_validator_effect: remove the known cross-root duplicate-id error for CM-0010 while preserving both claims

falsification_route: Fetch the pre- and post-migration blobs, verify the replay-protection record predates the peer-trigger record, and run node tools/continuity/validate-continuity-records.mjs. Reject this evidence if both records still resolve to CM-0010, if claim content was lost, or if the validator reports a different unresolved namespace collision.
